content.problemStatement:
You have a perfect binary tree with 2N2^N2N leaf nodes. A perfect binary tree is a rooted tree where all non-leaves have exactly 222 children, and all the leaves are equidistant from the root. It can be shown that there are exactly 2N+1‚ąí12^{N + 1} - 12N+1‚ąí1 total nodes then.

Here is an example with 888 leaf nodes and 151515 total nodes, i.e. N=3N = 3N=3.



You want to write binary integers (000 or 111) on each of the leaves. Then, you will calculate the integers written on the non-leaf nodes in the following format (recursively):


The value of a non-leaf node is the minimum of the values of the 222 children nodes.
Please find an assignment of values to the leaf nodes such that the sum of values in all the nodes (leaf or non-leaf) becomes exactly KKK. A solution may not exist as well, you have to report that.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of input of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, first output Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No depending on whether a solution exists. You may print each character in either case, i.e. YES\text{YES}YES, yes\text{yes}yes and yEs\text{yEs}yEs will all be accepted.


Only when the answer is Yes\text{Yes}Yes, on the next line, output a binary string SSS of length 2N2^N2N representing the assignment of 000 or 111 to the leaf nodes. We will compute the sum of values of all nodes, and that must be equal to KKK.


Formally, the perfect binary tree will be computed as follows:



The nodes are indexed from 111 to 2N+1‚ąí12^{N+1}-12N+1‚ąí1, where nodes 111 to 2N‚ąí12^N-12N‚ąí1 are non-leaf nodes.
The non-leaf node indexed iii is the parent of two nodes indexed 2‚čÖi2 \cdot i2‚čÖi and 2‚čÖi+12 \cdot i +12‚čÖi+1.
For all 1‚Č§i‚Č§2N1 \le i \le 2^N1‚Č§i‚Č§2N, SiS_iSi‚Äč is the value on the node indexed 2N‚ąí1+i2^N-1+i2N‚ąí1+i.
For all 1‚Č§i<2N1 \le i < 2^N1‚Č§i<2N, the value of node iii is minimum of values of nodes 2‚čÖi2 \cdot i2‚čÖi and 2‚čÖi+12 \cdot i + 12‚čÖi+1.
constraints: The sum of 2N2^N2N over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The values of nodes 222 and 333 are 000 as given by our binary string. The value of node 111 is minimum of value of node 222 and 333, which is again 000. Thus, the sum is 0+0+0=00 + 0 + 0 = 00+0+0=0.
Test Case 2 : The value of node 222 is 111 and the value of node 333 is 000, as given by our binary string. The value of node 111 is the minimum of the 222, i.e. 000. Thus, the sum is 0+1+0=10 + 1 + 0 = 10+1+0=1.
Test Case 3 : No valid construction exists with N=1,K=2N = 1, K = 2N=1,K=2.
